content: SAM2/SAM3 on Apple Silicon MPS backend has multiple documented correctness/compat issues: (1) RuntimeError 'Placeholder storage has not been allocated on MPS device!' on basic ops; (2) MPS does not support float64 (only float32/float16/bfloat16), so any code path defaulting to double precision throws TypeError and must be forced to float32; (3) pin_memory() + .to() interacts differently on MPS vs CUDA causing storage conflicts (SAM3/ultralytics issue #22954); (4) SAM3 specifically fails on MPS due to Triton kernel dependency (Triton has no MPS backend) — SAM2 is more MPS-viable than SAM3. Practical takeaway: force float32 everywhere, avoid pin_memory in the MPS dataloading path, and pin to SAM2 (not SAM3) for Mac-local inference.
